$schema_note: Machine- and human-readable cases from customer tickets about non-sensitive secret_* / naming false positives; use snippets with Trivy (trivy fs --scanners secret <path>), Aqua, or IDE rule debugging.

source.topic: FP: secret_key-style names with descriptive non-secret values (post SaaS upgrade)
references: Dockerfile RUN export quoted snake_case identifiers
reported categorization as password-in-plain-text in product UI

cases.title: Dockerfile RUN export quoted identifier value (ticket reproduction)
cases.customer_summary: RUN export secret_key="ssh_public_key" flags as sensitive after tenant upgrade.

expectation.description: Value is a descriptive identifier (snake_case), not entropy-heavy secret material.

cases.title: Shell script variant (hits passwords-in-plain-text path .sh)
cases.customer_summary: Same pattern as Dockerfile but analyzed as POSIX shell.

expectation.description: Same logical FP as CLIENT-FP-001 when scanners treat file as textual script.

cases.title: secret_path naming with non-sensitive path-ish value
cases.customer_summary: Keys describing paths or refs (similar class of FP as ticket).

expectation.description: Value looks like filesystem path segments, not a shared secret.

cases.title: Customer cited overpass/font-style key naming confusion
cases.customer_summary: Report mentioned 'overpassfont' style keys being surfaced as sensitive alongside secret_* proliferation.

expectation.description: Depends on detector: compound-word exclusions may apply only to specific key shapes; tracked as behavioral regression/regression harness.

cases.title: Still a real credential (same key shape — must remain detected)
cases.customer_summary: Sanity contrast: literal random-looking assignment should still match detectors.

expectation.description: Do not tune rules exclusively on key name — must preserve true positives.
